jurisdictional_context: Brazil's Lei nº 12.318/2010 (Lei da Alienação Parental, LAP) is the world's most explicit PA-recognition statute. Drafted by Elizio Luiz Perez (juiz TRT-2/SP), introduced as PL 4.053/2008 by dep. Régis de Oliveira (PSC-SP), rapporteur Sen. Paulo Paim (PT-RS), sanctioned 26 Aug 2010 (https://www.planalto.gov.br/ccivil_03/_ato2007-2010/2010/lei/l12318.htm). Amended by Lei 14.340/2022 (https://www.planalto.gov.br/ccivil_03/_ato2019-2022/2022/lei/l14340.htm) which removed automatic suspensão do poder familiar and made escuta especializada (Lei 13.431/2017) mandatory. CFP Nota Técnica 4/2022/GTEC/CG (https://site.cfp.org.br/wp-content/uploads/2022/08/SEI_CFP-0698871-Nota-Tecnica.pdf) instructs psicólogos not to ground analyses on Lei 12.318 and recognises gender bias. CFESS joint nota Dec 2022 (https://www.cfess.org.br/arquivos/nota-tecnica-LAP-2022-dez.pdf) extended critique to assistentes sociais. PL 1.372/2023 (full revocation) passed CCJ 37-28 on 3 Dec 2025; pendente Plenário. Psicólogos register with one of 24 CRPs under CFP (https://site.cfp.org.br/).

stance_codes.recognition: Treats AP as real, assessable; uses Lei 12.318/2010 framing; IBDFAM / PASG / GARI-PA aligned.
stance_codes.critique: Argues Lei 12.318/2010 scientifically weak (Gardner-derived SAP), gender-biased, weaponised against DV-survivor mothers / CSA-disclosing children; supports CFP NT 4/2022 and/or revocation.
stance_codes.middle: Recognises conflict dynamics exist but rejects AP/SAP as diagnostic; emphasises procedural safeguards (Resolução CFP 11/2018 depoimento especial).
stance_codes.institutional: Federal/regional regulators or conselhos with binding professional doctrine.

structural_findings: Brazil is the only LATAM jurisdiction where institutional critique camp (CFP NT 4/2022 + CFESS) and clinical recognition camp (Calçada / G. Brazil / Trindade / Molinari / Lobo Ribeiro / Karam) are roughly equal in institutional weight.
The 2017-2019 CFP debate was substantively dialogic — Sandra Baccara's recognition chapter inside CFP 2019 Debatendo documents this. Closure happened with NT 4/2022, not earlier.

Lei 14.340/2022 is genuinely ambiguous — recognition camp reads as procedural fine-tuning preserving LAP; critique camp reads as partial concession to femicide/DV-silencing critique. Both readings have textual support.

A small but important 'recognition-with-gender-asymmetry-concession' sub-camp exists (Lobo Ribeiro / Karam Trindade, Vida Mental SP) — they engage the 70.73%/75.6% accuser/accused gender split data on its own terms while maintaining AP can still be assessed.

Critique camp clinical roster is narrower than recognition camp, concentrated at UERJ + PUC-Rio (Arantes, Torraca de Brito, Martins de Sousa) plus Sedes Sapientiae SP (Pisano Motta). Without CFP + CFESS the practitioner critique camp would be visibly thinner.

CRP numbers are systematically under-published in BR. Only Calçada (CRP 05/18785), Lobo Ribeiro (CREMESP 114681) and Karam Trindade (CRP 06/205826) display registry numbers publicly. Direct CRP regional lookup will be needed for outreach verification on the other 11 entries.
PL 1.372/2023 (full revocation) passed CCJ 37-28 on 3 Dec 2025; pendente Plenário. Recognition camp is now openly fighting to preserve LAP — directory should expect significant churn through 2026.
